reasoning: 386 hanabi entries returned (real Wikipedia/Wikidata data), but most lack prefecture, coordinates, dates, and English names — the lang=en param had no effect on name translation. Scope is comprehensive but the output is nearly unusable for an English-speaking traveler planning a trip. RULE C: authoritative data present, practical gap is severe.
missing_data: English names for most items; dates/timing for each festival; prefecture coverage for ~70% of entries; crowd/access info.
improvement_hint: Enrich hanabi entries with name_en, prefecture, and event dates from Wikidata; filter/sort by those with most metadata when lang=en.

reasoning: Same 386 hanabi entries as L3-01 but lang=ja. Japanese user gets Japanese names which is correct. However dates, prefecture, and coordinates are still largely missing from most entries, making trip planning difficult. Authoritative data (RULE C) but low practical density.
missing_data: Event dates, prefecture codes, and coordinates for majority of entries.
improvement_hint: Same as L3-01: enrich Wikidata hanabi records with timing and location fields before serving.

reasoning: Query asks for snow festivals; tool filtered to Hokkaido but returned 0 festival items. The national_heritage fallback contains UNESCO intangible heritage from outside Hokkaido (Akita, Saitama, Toyama, Mie) — completely unrelated to snow festivals. Zero snow-festival data delivered. RULE A applies: wrong scope returned by server.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Sounkyo Ice Waterfall Festival, any Hokkaido yukimatsuri data.
wrong_data: Returned UNESCO items from Tohoku/Kanto/Chubu have no relation to Hokkaido snow festivals.
improvement_hint: Add snow-festival kind_tag (yukimatsuri) and ensure Hokkaido festive data is scraped; fallback should match theme not just national heritage.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for rural/country living experience (田舎暮らし体験). Tool called get_japan_heritage with lang=zh, which returns 104 Japan Heritage stories. Top canonical items (Jo-an teahouse, Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama Site) are heritage monuments, not rural-life experience spots. Data is real and authoritative but synthesized incorrectly to the intent. RULE C: authoritative but scope/intent gap.
missing_data: Rural guesthouses (minpaku), satoyama experience villages, traditional farming stays, agritourism sites.
improvement_hint: Route 田舎暮らし queries toward get_spots with kind_tags like satoyama, farm_stay, or rural_stay rather than Japan Heritage monuments.

reasoning: Query in Korean (단풍 = autumn leaves). Tool returned 140 results with real sakura/momiji spots. Results include coordinates, heritage designations, crowd bands. However lang=ko was passed but names and descriptions appear in English/Japanese, not Korean — constraint_handling -1 per RULE D. One dim penalized per RULE B.
missing_data: Korean-language names and descriptions for returned items.
improvement_hint: Ensure Korean translation pass covers primary attraction names; fall foliage timing data (peak weeks by region) would enhance usefulness.

reasoning: Query seeks quiet non-touristy meditation temples. search_area(修行) returned 88 results mixing temples, a ninja experience, and DMO pages. Top hits include Shugyō-ji Tokyo (named 修行 but generic), and Rinzai-ji zen experience in Shizuoka (¥3,000, practical info). Real data but query's 'not touristy' constraint is structurally unaddressable. Coverage of actual mountain ascetic temples (yamabushi, Koyasan shukubo) is sparse.
missing_data: Shukubo temple lodging options, Koyasan, Yamadera, Haguro shugendo sites.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo and yamabushi kind_tags; link to ascetic retreat programs from temple official pages.

reasoning: Query in Spanish asks for northern lights in Japan. Response correctly surfaces not_available block with factually accurate explanation (Japan is outside auroral oval, rare Hokkaido exceptions). Alt_kinds dark_sky/observatory provided with real named observatories. This is the infeasibility-promote pattern working well. Lang=es but response text in en/ja — RULE D applies, constraint_handling -1.
missing_data: Spanish-language explanation text.
improvement_hint: Translate not_available reason fields into requested lang; the alt suggestions are good and could include dark-sky park kind_tag entries.

reasoning: Query asks to sleep in a 100-year-old house (古民家). Tool called get_hotels(Gifu), returning onsen ryokan and generic hotels — no kominka/machiya or historic farmhouse accommodation. The query's core constraint (old historic building stay) is missed. Gifu scoping is reasonable (Shirakawa-go machiya exist), but the hotel data layer doesn't carry age/architectural-type filtering. RULE A: wrong tool class for the constraint.
missing_data: Kominka stays, machiya lodgings, Shirakawa-go gassho farmhouses (some offer lodging), heritage inn designations.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type: kominka/machiya to hotel records; enrich Wikidata hotel entities with construction era where available.

reasoning: Query in Thai (สถานที่ใหม่นอกจากการท่องเที่ยว = places off the tourist trail). Tool searched 桜 returning 215 sakura spots. Query's language is Thai and lang=th was passed but names appear in Japanese/English. Per RULE D constraint_handling -1. The sakura search is reasonable for spring off-beat spots but doesn't address 'non-touristy' aspect. Coverage of lesser-known spots is good via crowd_band=low items.
missing_data: Thai-language names; filtering logic to surface low-crowd sakura spots ahead of famous ones.
improvement_hint: Ensure Thai translation coverage; surface crowd_band=low items first when query implies avoiding tourists.

reasoning: Query asks for dramatic landscapes for photography. search_area(絶景) returned 53 results. Top hits are DMO article pages (Ehime feature routes, Niigata zekkei), not named specific landscape spots with coordinates. The real photogenic spots (Hakuba, Tottori dunes, Bitchu Matsuyama) are not in top results. Authoritative DMO data present (RULE C) but ranking surfaces listicle pages over named spots.
missing_data: Specific named photogenic locations with GPS coordinates; iconic viewpoints.
improvement_hint: Boost wikidata attraction results with coordinates over DMO article pages for 絶景 queries; add scenic_viewpoint kind_tag.

reasoning: Query in Arabic asks for open-air hot springs in nature (not hotels). Tool searched 露天風呂 returning 72 results with real onsen spots. Arabic lang=ar passed but text content in Japanese. RULE D: constraint_handling -1. The 'not in hotels' constraint is partially addressed — some results are standalone facilities. Results include URLs and descriptions (Japanese) that mention outdoor baths in nature, which partially satisfies the intent.
missing_data: Arabic-language descriptions; filtering to exclude hotel-attached baths; konyoku (mixed-gender) or wild onsen designations.
improvement_hint: Add Arabic translations for top onsen spots; add indoor_capable=outdoor filter priority for 露天 queries.

reasoning: Query asks to swim with wild dolphins in Japan. search_area(イルカ) returned 61 results. Top result is a Kyoto cultural ambassador page with no dolphin content. Second result is Notojiima Aquarium (captive dolphins, not wild). Third is a fish market. No wild dolphin swim locations (Mikurajima, Tatsunoshima). The query may be infeasibility-adjacent (swimming with wild dolphins is rare/regulated in Japan) but no not_available block was returned.
missing_data: Mikurajima dolphin snorkeling (recognized site), Tatsunoshima; infeasibility note for 'swimming with wild dolphins' being permit-regulated.
improvement_hint: Add not_available block for wild-dolphin-swimming queries explaining permit requirements; add dolphin_watching kind_tag for Ogasawara/Mikurajima.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional architecture untouched by modernization (重伝建 = preservation districts). Tool returned get_japan_heritage with 104 Japan Heritage stories. Top canonical items (Jo-an teahouse, Nagoya Castle, archaeological sites) are heritage monuments, not 重伝建 preservation townscapes. Data is real and authoritative but wrong category for the query. RULE C: 3 not 2 because heritage content is genuinely related.
missing_data: 重伝建 (Preservation Districts for Groups of Traditional Buildings): Tsumago, Shirakawa-go, Ouchi-juku, Kurashiki, Uchiko.
improvement_hint: Route 'traditional townscape' / 伝建 queries to search_area with preservation_district kind_tag or add a dedicated get_preservation_districts tool.

reasoning: Query in Vietnamese asks for Ainu cultural experience in Hokkaido. Tool correctly searched アイヌ and returned National Ainu Museum (Upopoy), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Kawamura Kaneto Museum — all accurate, Hokkaido-located, with coordinates. Excellent topical match. Lang=vi passed but results in English/Japanese names only (RULE D: constraint_handling -1). RULE B: single dim penalized.
missing_data: Vietnamese-language names/descriptions; Ainu cultural experience programs (craft workshops, dance performances).
improvement_hint: Prioritize Vietnamese translation for Hokkaido/Ainu content; add experience_type field to distinguish museum visits from participatory cultural events.

reasoning: Query asks for Buddhist cliff carvings (磨崖仏). search_area returned 73 results with Usuki Stone Buddhas (prominence 0.9, National Treasure), Ōya-dera (0.9), Komazaka Magaibutsu (Shiga), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima) — all real, named, with heritage designations and coordinates. Excellent topical precision. Minor caveat: some entries lack English names.
missing_data: English names for a few entries; access/transport info.
improvement_hint: Add name_en for remaining unnamed magaibutsu entries; enrich with visiting season recommendations.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for day-trip volcano hikes FROM TOKYO. search_area returned 88 results including Mt. Iwate (Tohoku), Mt. Fuji, Lake Towada, Mt. Unzen (Nagasaki) — many far from Tokyo and not day-trippable. The 'from Tokyo day trip' constraint is not honored; no distance/access filtering. Mt. Fuji is present which is valid but buried among non-feasible options for the stated constraint.
missing_data: Day-trip filter from Tokyo (Fuji, Hakone, Izu volcanoes); travel time / access info; safety/permit notes for active volcanoes.
improvement_hint: Add travel_feasibility routing for 'from [city]' constraints; promote Tokyo-region volcano results (Fuji, Mihara, Oshima) when origin is specified.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks to eat freshly made tofu at a temple (寺 豆腐 体験). search_area(豆腐) returned MAFF GI records (Iwayama frozen tofu, Isshuu tofu), a mountain-foot tofu shop, frozen tofu recipes — no temple tofu experience. Koyasan shojin ryori or Daitokuji temple tofu dining are entirely absent. Lang=id but results in Japanese. RULE D: constraint_handling -1. Data is real but wrong synthesis.
missing_data: Temple tofu dining experiences (Koyasan, Kyoto Daitokuji area, shojin ryori venues); Indonesian-language text.
improvement_hint: Add shojin_ryori and temple_food kind_tags to enable routing of temple-food queries; add Indonesian translation coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages frozen in time. search_area(漁港) returned 86 results. Top hit is a sakura-ebi restaurant article (Shizuoka DMO), second is Itoman fishing port (Okinawa, operational), third is a fish market street. Real preserved fishing villages (Tainoura in Chiba, Ine no Funaya in Kyoto, Himi in Toyama) are likely buried. Ranking surfaces active fishing ports and food articles over preserved historic ones.
missing_data: Ine-no-Funaya (Kyoto), preserved fishing hamlet entries; funaya/fishing_village kind_tag boosting.
improvement_hint: Boost fishing_village kind_tag entries in search ranking over DMO food articles for 漁港 queries; add preservation_district crosslink.

reasoning: Query asks where to learn traditional washi paper-making from a master. get_local_specialty(craft) returned 231 METI traditional craft records. Items visible are Ainu attus weaving, Oitama tsumugi, Ugo shina cloth — no washi (和紙) records visible in top results. Washi is a designated METI craft but not appearing at top. Scope is correct (craft category, authoritative METI data) but washi is buried or missing from visible portion. RULE C applies.
missing_data: Echizen washi, Mino washi, Tosa washi (all METI-designated); workshop/artisan experience venues.
improvement_hint: Add washi-specific keyword boosting; include experience_venue field linking to designated production areas offering tours.

reasoning: Query asks for places that look like period dramas (時代劇). search_area(宿場) returned 104 results. Top hits: Tsumago-juku (Important Preservation District, walking road, coordinates), Narai-juku, Seki-juku — all iconic post-town (shukuba) preservation districts with heritage designations. Excellent intent match. Municipal scrape results add rich narrative context about Kiso Road towns. Minor caveat: no explicit photo/filming location data.
missing_data: Ohara-juku, Magome-juku could be more prominent; actual film location tags would add value.
improvement_hint: Add film_location or period_drama kind_tag where DMO sources confirm it; shukuba results are already strong.

reasoning: Query asks for uncrowded beaches. search_area(ビーチ) returned 115 results. Top results are Okinawa beaches (Naminoue, Sunayama, Tropical Beach) which are popular/medium-crowd. The 'not crowded' constraint is unaddressed — crowd_band data exists in entries but is not used for filtering/ranking. Several entries show crowd_band=medium. RULE B single-dim penalty for constraint_handling.
missing_data: crowd_band=low filtering to surface genuinely less-visited beaches; mainland Japan / hidden island beaches.
improvement_hint: Apply crowd_band=low priority when query contains 'not crowded' / 穴場 signals; surface remote island beaches (Amami, Ogasawara) for uncrowded intent.

reasoning: Query asks for small old-school izakaya yokocho. search_area(横丁) returned 74 results. Top hit is 横丁公園 (a park, not a drinking alley). Second is a Chinese-language Saga tourism page. Haikara't Yokocho (amusement park) and ramen yokocho appear — but real izakaya yokocho (Yurakucho, Harmonica Yokocho, Ebisu Yokocho, Noren Yokocho Sapporo) are likely buried. Ranking failure — park and tourism article surface above actual drinking alleys.
missing_data: Harmonica Yokocho (Kichijoji), Yurakucho Corridor, Noren Yokocho (Sapporo), Shinbashi Yakitori Alley.
improvement_hint: Boost yokocho kind_tag wikidata entries over parks that happen to contain 横丁 in name; add izakaya_street as a sub-kind.

reasoning: Query in German asks for rural Japan train travel. search_area(ローカル線) returned 74 results. Top hits: Sagano Scenic Line (Kyoto), Oigawa Main Line (Shizuoka, steam trains), Nagano local railway narrative — all accurate and relevant with real names. Excellent topical match. Lang=de passed but results in English/Japanese (RULE D: constraint_handling -1). Otherwise strong response.
missing_data: German-language names/descriptions; timetable or access hints.
improvement_hint: Extend German translation coverage to local_railway kind entries; add journey duration and scenic highlights fields.

reasoning: Query asks for firefly viewing spots in Japan. search_area(ホタル) returned 73 results. Top hit is ホタルイカ群遊海面 (firefly squid, not firefly insects) — a Named Natural Monument but off-topic. Second is ホタルの里 park (Hyogo), third is 美郷のホタル (Tokushima, Natural Monument). Fourth is Toyama Firefly Museum. Fifth is Kitakyushu firefly viewing page. Then Yamaguchi firefly boat ride with dates. Real data, good specificity. Minor: first hit is squid not insect firefly.
missing_data: Firefly season timing (June); ranking should de-prioritize ホタルイカ (squid) for 'firefly viewing' queries.
improvement_hint: Disambiguate ホタル (insect) vs ホタルイカ (squid) at query time; add seasonal_viewing kind_tag with month windows.

reasoning: Query in Russian asks where to see cranes in winter in Japan. search_area(鶴) returned 216 results. Top hit is 出水ツル渡来地 (Izumi crane migration grounds, Kagoshima) — the #1 real answer, Special Natural Monument, Ramsar site, with coordinates and accurate description about 10,000 cranes Oct–March. Kushiro-shitsugen also present (tancho crane habitat). However lang=ru passed but all names in English/Japanese (RULE D: constraint_handling -1). Many municipality entries with 鶴 in name dilute results.
missing_data: Russian-language names/descriptions; Kushiro tancho crane reserve specifics; viewing season emphasis.
improvement_hint: Filter out municipality-name matches when attraction-class crane habitat exists; extend Russian translation to top wildlife-viewing spots.
